name: recon
tagline: Network reconnaissance toolkit — HTTP, TLS, DNS, and a full Rhai script engine.
description: Started as a curl clone and grew into a multi-protocol investigation tool. Covers HTTP(S), TLS certificate inspection, DNS, WHOIS, ping, traceroute, archive handling, and Markdown/HTML/PDF conversion. The Rhai script engine exposes every probe and helper for automation.

name: sqlt
tagline: Multi-dialect SQL parser and translator for MySQL, MariaDB, Postgres, MSSQL, and SQLite.
description: Parses SQL into a JSON AST, emits SQL back from JSON, and translates between dialects. Early development; see CHANGELOG.md and OUT-OF-SCOPE.md in the repo.

name: tess
tagline: A less-style terminal pager with structured-log filtering and pretty-printing.
description: A pager for files, pipes, and live logs with first-class structured-log filtering, pretty-printing of JSON/YAML/TOML/XML/HTML/CSV, and wrap-aware scrolling. macOS + Linux.

name: batty
tagline: A cat / bat clone with syntax highlighting, git diff markers, and a bundled Rhai grammar.
description: Includes an interactive TUI mode, vim-style relative line numbers, Markdown rendering, tail/follow mode, and a TOML config. Targets macOS and Linux.

name: webrunner
tagline: Zero-config development web server with CGI and .htaccess support.
description: Static files, directory listings, classic CGI scripts (Perl/PHP/TypeScript via bun), URL rewriting, basic auth, custom error pages, and self-signed HTTPS — driven by familiar Apache-style .htaccess files.

name: nrg
tagline: A REPL-style wrapper for `npm run` and `git`, with passthru for most of the everyday shell tools.
description: Short for NPM Run + Git — the two CLIs I use most. nrg wraps both, exposes a small REPL for project-scoped sessions, and passes through familiar shell utilities (cat, cp, curl, awk, etc.) so you rarely have to leave it.

name: nrun
tagline: `npm run` with a few quality-of-life improvements.
description: A small Go wrapper around `npm run` — fewer keystrokes, nicer output, and a handful of features that turn the daily script-runner into something pleasant.

name: dynamicconfig
tagline: Layered configuration for Node — files and environment, with fallbacks, defaults, and optional throw-on-missing.
description: Dynamic configuration storage that reads from files and/or environment, supports multiple named config sets, falls back to a default config file when present, and lets each lookup either return a default or throw on missing keys. TypeScript declarations included; published as @codedv8/dynamicconfig on npm.

name: inmemcache
tagline: A small in-memory local cache, written in TypeScript.
description: Memory-based local cache. Small, focused, no dependencies. Written in TypeScript.

name: ts-totype
tagline: A self-confessed bad-practice helper for casting to `any` or `unknown` when the linter says no.
description: A workaround for the times you really do need to cast to `any`, `unknown`, or another type the linting rules forbid. Honest about being a bad practice; useful when nothing else fits.

name: configish
tagline: Configurable, dynamic config objects — small and focused.
description: A config object that is configurable and dynamic.
